name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Close the loophole
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Dear friend,
This week, Senators Chris Murphy, Elizabeth Warren, and dozens of others took to the Senate floor to make a bold stand for common sense gun safety legislation. During this filibuster, they spoke for more than 14 hours about why we must close the loophole in our law that allows suspected terrorists to buy guns and explosives.
I watched with pride when our senior Senator and my friend Jeanne Shaheen took to the floor to do her part. However, one person I did not see was Senator Kelly Ayotte.
Unfortunately, Senator Ayotte has voted _against_ common sense gun safety legislation and _with_ her special interest backers time and time again. I hope this time will be different. [**Join me in asking Senator Ayotte to stop putting the gun lobby ahead of the safety of Granite Staters and vote for real gun safety legislation.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd4df/2a380d31/137032ea/fbc660/1695138207/VEsH/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/sign-we-need-common-sense-gun-safety-legislation-nh?&source=e20160616_safety_NH")
We've seen too many tragedies to sit by and let loopholes like this remain open. It's past time to come together, put aside partisan rhetoric, and take action to ensure that dangerous individuals don't have access to these kinds of weapons.
[**If you agree, please put your name down to join me and tell Senator Ayotte -- enough. It's time to vote in the best interests of New Hampshire families and pass common sense gun safety legislation. Sign now.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd4df/2a380d31/137032ea/fbc660/1695138207/VEsE/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/sign-we-need-common-sense-gun-safety-legislation-nh?&source=e20160616_safety_NH")
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

Peter, This is a BIG DEAL: With your help, EVERY child in Indiana could soon have access to pre-Kindergarten education. Yesterday, State Superintendent of Public Instruction Glenda Ritz and I announced our plan to make it happen. Now we need your help to build public pressure on our state's leaders to give our kids a shot at success. **[Sign my petition now and tell Indiana leaders:"Every Hoosier child deserves access to pre-K education!"](https://act.myngp.com/el/5022617046601828352/2755780260355508224?utm_campaign=adv_prekem_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160616advprekemgqfuljg&refcode=em16160616advprekemgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Two years ago, Governor Pence failed Hoosier kids by declining an $80 million federal grant to give them pre-K education. And with your help, we'll expand the program even further to give every child access. But we can't give these kids a chance at a brighter future without building momentum now. name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Link of the Week: Remembering Orlando
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
_This is our Link of the Week email - the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about._
This week is a little different -- After the deadliest mass shooting in American history ended 49 lives too soon, we grieve with Orlando, the LGBT community, and our entire nation for the victims of this despicable act of terror and hate.
The victims were a couple who had just bought their first home; a mother who went out dancing with her son; an incredible singer; students and lawyers and members of the military; spouses and siblings and family. They were loved and they will be missed. They are remembered.
We remember their names, celebrate their lives, and commit ourselves to never letting hate win. You can join us by reading more about their lives **[here.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd4dd/2a380d31/136b50d4/fbc64a/3049838934/VEsH/ "http://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2016/06/12/481785763/heres-what-we-know-about-the-orlando-shooting-victims")**
As we strive to recover and find a way forward, we must, as Maggie said, "continue fighting for inclusiveness and ensure that all LGBT Americans can live free from fear..." [**Learn more about how you can join in the fight by volunteering or donating to The Center, Orlando's LGBT community space.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd4dd/2a380d31/136b50d4/fbc64b/3049838934/VEsE/ "http://www.thecenterorlando.org/")
In unity,

Team Maggie

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

Liberal Jim Justice thinks the rules don’t apply to him. **That’s not a risk West Virginia can afford to take.** The restrictive policies and job-killing agenda that the Obama administration has implemented will be continued by Jim Justice and Hillary Clinton.

**The Justice and Clinton Team must be stopped.**

Our message is simple, but important, to turn West Virginia around:
* Improve West Virginia’s economy in order to encourage investment and create jobs

* Reform education to focus our investments on the students rather than funding a bloated administration

* Invest in West Virginia’s infrastructure including statewide broadband to encourage business growth as well as educational opportunities

* Fight against drug addiction, find treatment for those that need help, and create a jobs environment that will help our youth find gainful employment before they find drugs

With your help, West Virginia can become a state that will attract economic growth and offer a brighter future for all West Virginians. A win in the governor’s race will send a message that will resonate not only across West Virginia but America.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Scott?=
subject: Your Campaign Update
body:
| | | Your Weekly Campaign Update
---
[View this email in your browser](http://us13.campaign-archive2.com/?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=55d7b40b61&e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| Greetings,
---
| Phil continued introducing additional specific plans this week to ensure Vermonters understand his approach to leadership and how he’ll move Vermont forward. That work didn’t keep him tied down in the office, however, as he continued to hit the road, touring places of work and visiting with hundreds of Vermonters. A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below.

**PHIL LAYS OUT HIS BUDGET POLICY & PROPOSALS FOR FISCAL RESPONSIBILITY**

Phil introduced his state budget policy plan this week, outlining his plan and proposals for **establishing truth in state budgeting and real fiscal responsibility** as Governor.

The plan details specific proposals for **prioritizing economic growth and affordability, setting clear budget limits and strengthening Vermont’s workforce through pro-growth investments.** Phil will be a leader that is honest about what things cost, what we can afford and the need to eliminate inefficiencies within state government. His plan will establish a transparent, outcome-oriented and fiscally responsible state budget. Read the full plan [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c3ce4dc3b9&e=19cebfe173).
***
**REMINDER: PRIMARY VOTING HAS OFFICIALLY STARTED – VOTE EARLY FOR PHIL!**

Early voting for the August 9 primary opened this week for residents of Barre Town, and **will open in _all Vermont towns by June 24_**. We strongly encourage all Vermonters to check in with your town clerk’s office to identify the date for early voting in your town. Your vote is your voice, so be sure to know the rules and **_get out the vote_**! You can view contact information for your town clerk’s office [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=88ff5927b6&e=19cebfe173).
***
**VERMONT NEEDS A MECHANIC, AND PHIL IS JUST THE PERSON FOR THE JOB**
We all know how hard Phil works, and his diligent, meticulous approach to listening to constituents and learning more by going “under the hood” of each issue. That work ethic and commitment to Vermonters was documented in an article, “Phil Scott, the Quintessential Mechanic,” by Mark Johnston for VT Digger, and we encourage you to read the full piece [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6616326507&e=19cebfe173).

As highlighted in the article, Phil has always been driven by a strong work ethic, and a mechanic’s mindset when it comes to solving problems. **He’ll “take [an issue] apart, examine the parts, fix it and put it back together.”** This is the exact approach needed by our next governor, who we all know, will face tremendous challenges.

Those that know Phil best, including family, colleagues and business partners, shared more insight into Phil’s leadership qualities, and Phil elaborated on his approach to addressing our most serious challenge: affordability.

**“Scott says high property****taxes are the biggest reason Vermont is unaffordable. Add on increasing government fees, and he says Vermonters are like frogs in the frying pan.** One idea he promotes is to increase the state’s population to 700,000 to spread the costs of government more broadly. In particular, he wants to recruit younger people. Asked how, he suggests offering 20- and 30-year-olds a break on their state income tax.” Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=97c3b8f9e8&e=19cebfe173).

***
**HEARING YOUR VOICE!**
As Phil told VT Digger this week, when it comes to working hard “you just keep going.” That commitment rings true, as **Phil and Team Scott continue to meet with Vermonters and visit our amazing small business community throughout the state.** It’s so important to Phil that he hear directly from Vermonters about the challenges they face and how he can help solve them. Thanks, as always, to everyone who welcomed Team Scott this week, and shared your stories and ideas for growing our economy and making Vermont more affordable! With stops in Arlington, Northshire, Charlotte, Bradford, Manchester, Sharon, Barre and more, Phil met with local residents and toured businesses, including Carved Solutions, Equinox Valley Nursery, Farm-Way, Miles Lumber Company & Miles Fuels, Newton Pellet, Sandy’s Drive-in Restaurant, Spear’s Corner Store, and Wilcox Ice Cream. Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT
We’d love to meet you at our next stop, so check out our full listing of events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8c58b01c63&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_“Support Phil Scott”_**
“Phil Scott has remained here as legislator and Lt. Governor, attempting to stem the bleeding of the state’s investments." – Christopher Maloney, Washington (Source: Times Argus: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=65da2f8161&e=19cebfe173))

**_Mourning with the entire nation following the Orlando tragedy_**
We, along with our entire country, were heartbroken by the tragedy in Orlando, and our thoughts are with all of the victims and their families. Phil joined other local leaders in sharing his condolences. “It's difficult to find the words to express our collective national shock and grief over this senseless act of violence.” (Source: WPTZ: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e5c42e80c3&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Jeff Couture in VT Digger: Tech Jobs Represent an Economic Growth Opportunity_**
In the budget policy plan Phil put forth this week, he proposed a strategy to invest in people – to invest in areas that build and strengthen our workforce, which will grow our economy and allow businesses to thrive with talented, qualified labor. As Phil has said, nurturing our growing tech industry and bolstering STEM education programs will be a critical element to growing our economy. The strength and value of that industry was highlighted by Vermont Technology Alliance Executive Director Jeff Couture in VT Digger this week, which you can read [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0b7b6703e9&e=19cebfe173). Read more about Phil’s plans to grow our economy by investing in pro-growth areas, such as tech, [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e94606aaf2&e=19cebfe173).

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=06d93f1647&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=aa34513652&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

That work ethic and commitment to Vermonters was documented in an article, “Phil Scott, the Quintessential Mechanic,” by Mark Johnston for VT Digger, and we encourage you to read the full piece . As highlighted in the article, Phil has always been driven by a strong work ethic, and a mechanic’s mindset when it comes to solving problems. He’ll “take [an issue] apart, examine the parts, fix it and put it back together.” This is the exact approach needed by our next governor, who we all know, will face tremendous challenges. Those that know Phil best, including family, colleagues and business partners, shared more insight into Phil’s leadership qualities, and Phil elaborated on his approach to addressing our most serious challenge: affordability. “Scott says high property**taxes are the biggest reason Vermont is unaffordable. Add on increasing government fees, and he says Vermonters are like frogs in the frying pan. One idea he promotes is to increase the state’s population to 700,000 to spread the costs of government more broadly. In particular, he wants to recruit younger people. Asked how, he suggests offering 20- and 30-year-olds a break on their state income tax.” Read the full article . * HEARING YOUR VOICE! As Phil told VT Digger this week, when it comes to working hard “you just keep going.” That commitment rings true, as Phil and Team Scott continue to meet with Vermonters and visit our amazing small business community throughout the state. It’s so important to Phil that he hear directly from Vermonters about the challenges they face and how he can help solve them. Thanks, as always, to everyone who welcomed Team Scott this week, and shared your stories and ideas for growing our economy and making Vermont more affordable! With stops in Arlington, Northshire, Charlotte, Bradford, Manchester, Sharon, Barre and more, Phil met with local residents and toured businesses, including Carved Solutions, Equinox Valley Nursery, Farm-Way, Miles Lumber Company & Miles Fuels, Newton Pellet, Sandy’s Drive-in Restaurant, Spear’s Corner Store, and Wilcox Ice Cream. Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT We’d love to meet you at our next stop, so check out our full listing of events . ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS “Support Phil Scott” “Phil Scott has remained here as legislator and Lt. Governor, attempting to stem the bleeding of the state’s investments." – Christopher Maloney, Washington (Source: Times Argus: ) Mourning with the entire nation following the Orlando tragedy We, along with our entire country, were heartbroken by the tragedy in Orlando, and our thoughts are with all of the victims and their families. Phil joined other local leaders in sharing his condolences. “It's difficult to find the words to express our collective national shock and grief over this senseless act of violence.” (Source: WPTZ: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS Jeff Couture in VT Digger: Tech Jobs Represent an Economic Growth Opportunity** In the budget policy plan Phil put forth this week, he proposed a strategy to invest in people – to invest in areas that build and strengthen our workforce, which will grow our economy and allow businesses to thrive with talented, qualified labor. As Phil has said, nurturing our growing tech industry and bolstering STEM education programs will be a critical element to growing our economy. The strength and value of that industry was highlighted by Vermont Technology Alliance Executive Director Jeff Couture in VT Digger this week, which you can read . Read more about Phil’s plans to grow our economy by investing in pro-growth areas, such as tech, . Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – and . Ask Hillary a question | Friend --

Have you always wanted to ask Hillary a question? Maybe something serious -- like her plans to support military families? Or something silly -- like the details about her favorite vacation?

This might be your chance: **[Sign up now for a special call with Hillary on Monday, June 20th, just for supporters like you.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=15&ms=MjU3NzE5MjgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIyNzA0ODA2S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

The upcoming call is a chance to check in as we celebrate a historic milestone -- this team has done some incredible work together over the last year! But we’ve got to be ready to take on a terrifying opponent in the general election, and there’s a lot left still to do. On the call, you’ll get a chance to hear directly from Hillary about where we stand, what we’ve accomplished, and what lies ahead for us (or whatever else is on your mind). She’ll also answer a few questions, so send yours in when you RSVP.

**Sign up right now to join Hillary on the phone on Monday -- and don’t forget to submit your question, too!**
